preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a form of fortition or hardening. It typically occurs through yod-coalescence in rapid speech, where the friction of the [ʃ] is preceded by a momentary stop closure (often from a neighboring [t]), resulting in a complete affricate.

examples.note: Coalescence where the sibilant and glide merge into a hardened affricate.
Historical hardening of postalveolar fricatives into affricates in specific word-initial positions.
